name: El Celemín Route
name_original: El Celemín por el Embalse de Benamarías – circular desde Benamarías
description: Hike the moderate El Celemín Route, a 7.9-mile circular trail through oak forests, past reservoirs, and offering views of the Porcos valley.
region_intro: Hiking around Magaz De Cepeda offers diverse landscapes, from gentle valley paths to challenging mountainous sections. The region features lush woodlands with poplar, alder, and willow trees at lower elevations, transitioning to broom, pine, and oak forests at higher altitudes. Notable natural features include the area's only natural lagoon and the Benamarías reservoir, providing varied scenery for outdoor exploration. This varied terrain ensures a range of hiking experiences for different preferences. Best hiking trails around Magaz De Cepeda The most popular hiking route is El Celemín Route, a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 27 minutes to complete. This moderate path leads through oak forests and offers views of the Porcos valley. Another top favourite among local hikers is Laguna Gallega via the vega of the Tuerto river – circular from Villamejil, a moderate 11.0 miles (17.7 km) path. This route explores the natural lagoon, combining natural exploration with historical elements. Local hikers also love the Murias de Rechivaldo – Santa Catalina de Somoza loop from Castrillo de los Polvazares, a 10.0 miles (16.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 12 minutes. Hiking around Magaz De Cepeda is defined by varied terrain, lush valleys, oak forests, and unique natural features like its lagoon and reservoir. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Magaz De Cepeda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Magaz De Cepeda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails can I explore in Magaz De Cepeda?
A: Magaz De Cepeda offers a diverse network of over 40 hiking trails. These routes cater to various preferences, ranging from easy strolls through valleys to more challenging mountainous sections.

Q: Are there easy hiking routes suitable for beginners in Magaz De Cepeda?
A: Yes, Magaz De Cepeda has several easy routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. There are 10 easy trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38164084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ecce Homo Hermitage – Murias de Rechivaldo loop from Astorga Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;, which is just under 7 km long and features minimal elevation gain.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter on the trails in Magaz De Cepeda?
A: The region boasts varied terrain. You'll find lush woodlands with poplar, alder, and willow trees in the valleys, transitioning to broom, pine, and oak forests at higher altitudes. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050878090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Celemín Route&lt;/a&gt; specifically lead through beautiful oak forests, offering views of the Porcos valley.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Magaz De Cepeda?
A: Yes, many routes in Magaz De Cepeda are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050892817" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Gallega via the vega of the Tuerto river – circular from Villamejil&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the region's unique natural lagoon.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Hikers can discover several notable natural features. The region is home to its only natural lagoon, accessible via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050892817" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Gallega via the vega of the Tuerto river – circular from Villamejil&lt;/a&gt;. The Benamarías reservoir is another significant landmark, passed by trails such as the 'Ruta Todo Monte'.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions near the hiking areas?
A: Absolutely. The region around Magaz De Cepeda is rich in history. You can visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5898729" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of Saint Mary of Astorga&lt;/a&gt; or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4637297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Episcopal Palace of Astorga (Gaudí Palace)&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4953546" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ecce Homo Hermitage&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable religious site.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Magaz De Cepeda?
A: The hiking trails in Magaz De Cepeda are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the continuous and beautiful changes in landscape, from shaded valleys to scenic mountain sections.

Q: Are the trails in Magaz De Cepeda suitable for families with children?
A: Many of the easier and moderate trails are suitable for families. The varied landscapes and natural features like the lagoon can make for an engaging experience for children. Consider shorter, flatter routes like the 'Ruta Contrastes' for a pleasant family outing.

Q: Is it possible to hike with dogs on the trails in Magaz De Cepeda?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in Magaz De Cepeda. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to carry water for them, particularly on longer routes or during warmer months.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Magaz De Cepeda?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the best times for hiking in Magaz De Cepeda, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, so early morning hikes are advisable. Winter hiking is possible, but some higher routes might require appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking more demanding terrain, there are challenging options. While most routes are moderate, trails like the 'Ruta Todo Monte' feature tough sections with continuous changes in landscape, providing a rewarding experience. There is 1 difficult trail available in the region.

Q: What should I consider regarding water availability on longer routes?
A: On some longer or more remote routes, water sources can be scarce. For example, on the 'Ruta Todo Monte', beyond the Benamarías reservoir, resources can be limited until Ucedo. It's always advisable to carry sufficient water, especially during warmer weather or on extended hikes.
tips: The route is approximately 12 kilometres long and of low difficulty, making it accessible to most hikers. It is a perfect place to enjoy nature and disconnect from the daily hustle and bustle.

The Benamarías reservoir is a lovely place to enjoy a day outdoors. It is located in the town of Benamarías, in the region of La Cepeda, Castilla y León. This reservoir is ideal for activities such as hiking, fishing and, of course, enjoying a nice picnic in its picnic area.
The picnic area has tables and benches, making it a perfect place to relax and enjoy nature. In addition, the surroundings offer beautiful views and a peaceful atmosphere, ideal for disconnecting from the daily hustle and bustle.

The picnic area has tables and benches, making it a perfect place to relax and enjoy nature. In addition, the surroundings offer beautiful views and a peaceful atmosphere, ideal for disconnecting from the daily hustle and bustle.

faqs.question: Where does the El Celemín Route start, and is parking available?
faqs.answer: The route primarily starts in Benamarías, a village in Magaz de Cepeda, León, Spain. Parking is generally available in or near the village, as it's a common starting point for local trails.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hiking route?
faqs.answer: The El Celemín Route is considered to have a low difficulty, with gentle ascents. It's a circular route, approximately 12 kilometers long, making it suitable for most hikers.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll traverse paths through oak trees, pass by the La Raldona reservoir, and experience gently ascending terrain amidst ancient drylands. The landscape evolves to include brooms, heather, and reforested pine forests, offering views over the Porcos valley. You'll also pass through Zacos village, known for its traditional architecture.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Highlights include the Path Through the Oak Forest, the Embalse de Benamarías, and a Picnic Area at Benamarías Reservoir. The route also offers glimpses of Zacos village with its traditional architecture, an hermitage, and a Romanesque church. The highest point, El Celemín (1,136 meters), gives the route its name.

faqs.question: Is the El Celemín Route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, trails of this nature in rural Spain are generally dog-friendly. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially near livestock or in protected natural areas, and to carry water for them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers different aromas and sights throughout the seasons due to its ancient dryland fields. Spring and autumn are generally ideal for hiking in this region, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, while winter might bring colder conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the El Celemín Route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What kind of flora and fauna might I encounter?
faqs.answer: You can observe local flora such as the Pyrenean oak (Quercus pyrenaica), well-adapted to dry, continental climates. The changing landscape throughout the year also showcases brooms, heather, and reforested pine forests. While specific fauna isn't detailed, expect typical woodland and rural wildlife.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its low difficulty and gentle ascents, the El Celemín Route is generally suitable for families with children who are accustomed to hiking. The circular nature and points of interest like the reservoir and picnic area add to its appeal for a family outing.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation nearby?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near villages like Benamarías and Zacos. While specific cafes or accommodations aren't detailed for the immediate trailhead, you can typically find basic services in these small towns or in larger nearby municipalities in the León province.

faqs.question: How does this 'El Celemín Route' differ from other trails with similar names?
faqs.answer: This specific El Celemín Route is a hiking trail located in Magaz de Cepeda, León, Spain. It's important not to confuse it with the 'Celemín Footbridge' in Cádiz, which is part of cycling routes, or the 'Túnel de Medio Celemín' in Valdemanco, a dark, disused railway tunnel used for various activities.
